nodes.title: Five Community Non-Profits Receive over $226,000 from The PM Group
Thomas J. Henry Supports Battered Women and Children During Child Abuse Prevention Month
Rudy’s Country Store & Bar-B-Q Raises $117K for SA Cancer Council

pageContext.content:
When first responders lose their life in the line of duty, The 100 Club of San Antonio provides funds to their family for immediate and long-term needs. That’s why The PM Group shows our support to those first responders who dedicate their careers (and their lives) to keep us safe and protected from harm’s way.

 

 

Since 1973, when Law Enforcement Officers and Firefighters pay the ultimate sacrifice with their life, The 100 Club of San Antonio is there. Funds are available to the family for immediate needs, placing a strong emphasis on higher education funding for surviving dependent children.

 

 

The 100 Club is citizen-based and not affiliated with any police, fire or governmental agency. They serve all Local, State and Federal Law-Enforcement and Fire Departments in Bexar County and its municipalities. If any law-enforcement officer, agent or firefighter is based in Bexar County, The 100 Club of San Antonio will be there to support them and their family.

 

 

Their primary goal is to deliver funds to the family within 24 to 48 hours of their loss in order to help with any immediate expenses that will most undoubtedly arise upon the unexpected death of their loved one. Afterward, they continue to support the family and place any dependent children into The 100 Club Survivor Fund, where there are currently 12 Survivor Children receiving five year monthly annuity payments to assist with their education, and an additional 18 minor children in line to receive their assistance.

 

 

That’s why all of our affiliated companies proudly support The 100 Club. The PM Group’s President and Chief Operating Officer Fran Yanity recently presented the organization with a check for $10,000 to help them in their mission to assist the families in our community who have lost a loved one.

 

 

“The PM Group has a long history of supporting The 100 Club and the brave men and woman who make our community a safer place to live. We are honored to do our part in helping them fulfill their mission,” stated Bob Wills, The PM Group’s CEO and Founder.  “When you become the largest advertising and marketing firm in San Antonio, you have an obligation to give back to the community that has done so much for you,” stated Yanity.

 

 

 

 

About The PM Group: The PM Group was founded in 1988. For over 33 years, the agency has continued to grow and is San Antonio’s largest marketing and advertising agency. The PM Group’s client roster spans numerous industries including home services, automotive, CPG, entertainment and sports marketing, legal, business services, non-profits, real estate and fitness. For two consecutive years now, the Agency has set annual revenue records with the addition of six new clients and through the continued success of our existing clients. Additionally, the agency hosts their annual Charity Gala that to date has raised millions of dollars for local San Antonio area non-profit organizations in the last 15 years! In 2020 alone, the Agency donated over $1,050,000 to local non-profits. It is our way of giving back to the community that we so humbly serve.
